commentary_text: The model assigns Japan a 69% win probability, while the sportsbook consensus puts it at 45%—a 24-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in a Japanese victory. This divergence suggests the market is pricing in information the model's Elo-based framework cannot access: recent lineup changes, injury status, or current form in the lead-up to the match could be weighing on market confidence despite Japan's historical strength relative to Sweden. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal worth monitoring; it does not indicate which assessment will prove more accurate when the teams take the pitch.
